name: Nid-Hca Reeves
description_short: HUD-approved housing counseling agency providing free guidance on homeownership, financial stability, and housing solutions through certified counselors and educational workshops.
description_long:
NID Housing Counseling Agency is a HUD-approved intermediary organization operating as a national network of advocacy groups, housing counselors, real estate professionals, community organizations, and faith-based groups. The agency was established to empower individuals and communities through comprehensive housing solutions and sustainable homeownership initiatives across the nation.

The organization provides free housing counseling services delivered by HUD-certified counselors, homebuyer workshops, financial stability guidance, and down payment assistance navigation. They also offer counselor training programs, host workshops and webinars on affordable housing and community development, and collaborate with educational institutions (HUCBUs/MSIs) on student housing solutions. All services are provided at no cost to clients, with a stated 48-hour response commitment for inquiries.

NID-HCA distinguishes itself through its multi-stakeholder network model combining advocacy, education, and resource provision rather than operating as a single counseling office. They explicitly remain operational during government disruptions (as noted in their government shutdown statement) and provide personalized guidance documented through client success stories—including cases of clients achieving homeownership with down payment assistance as substantial as $80,000.

As a non-profit HUD-approved agency, NID-HCA is fundamentally limited to free educational and counseling services rather than direct lending or credit repair. While their mission and client testimonials demonstrate genuine impact, the website provides limited operational details about service scope, counselor availability by location, or specific eligibility requirements beyond geographic service areas.
pros: HUD-approved intermediary status provides regulatory legitimacy and oversight
All services explicitly free with no hidden fees or charges
HUD-certified counselors on staff for professional guidance
Documented client success stories showing substantial outcomes (e.g., $80,000 down payment assistance, first-home closings)
Multi-stakeholder network model connecting clients to advocacy groups, real estate professionals, and community organizations
Operates as national network with local branches and 48-hour response commitment
Offers both one-on-one counseling and group workshops for diverse learning preferences
cons: Website does not clearly specify service availability by location or which branches serve specific regions
Limited details on counselor qualifications, experience levels, or specialties beyond HUD certification
No information about service capacity, wait times, or availability of same-day/urgent counseling
Existing client portal mentioned but no description of how long services typically last or follow-up support structure
No explicit information about debt counseling or credit repair capabilities—appears primarily housing-focused
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking education on mortgage products and homeownership requirements
Individuals and families pursuing down payment assistance programs and housing subsidies
People rebuilding financial stability after housing or economic hardship
Housing counselors and professionals seeking training and certification development
diagnosis: NID-HCA is best for first-time homebuyers, individuals seeking down payment assistance, and those rebuilding financial stability—particularly in underserved communities. The main caveat is that this is a counseling and education agency only; they do not provide direct loans, credit repair, or emergency cash assistance, and service availability by location requires contacting them directly.
services: Homebuyer workshops and financial literacy education
Down payment assistance program navigation and qualification support
Housing counselor training and certification programs
Workshops and webinars on affordable housing and community development
Advocacy and education on housing access and mortgage product improvement
Partnerships with educational institutions (HBCUs/MSIs) on student housing solutions
Financial stability counseling and goal-setting support
Community development and neighborhood resilience initiatives
Government advocacy and communications on housing disparities
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 4602 Crenshaw Blvd Ste 2A, , Los Angeles, CA 90043
